summary: Matched the ditto/poison mushroom item module `itdkinoko` by replacing the 305-line assembly file with a 148-line C implementation, expanding the header prototypes, and moving the object from asm/nonmatching to src/matching in the build configuration. The implementation reconstructs the item state table, spawn/setup behavior, ground/air state callbacks, collision response, velocity handling, and destroy/interaction wrapper behavior using existing item helpers and shared Kinoko attributes.
what_changed: Deleted `asm/melee/it/items/itdkinoko.s`, removing the old hand-assembled implementation for functions `it_80293A08` through `it_80293D74` and data symbol `it_803F6130`.
Added a full C implementation in `src/melee/it/items/itdkinoko.c` including the two-entry `ItemStateTable it_803F6130[]`.
Implemented initialization in `it_80293A08`, including facing direction based on `it->pos.x`, clearing `xD5C`, initializing `xDD4_itemVar.kinoko` fields, and transitioning with `it_80293BE8`.

Implemented movement/state callbacks for grounded and aerial behavior using existing item helpers such as `it_8026B390`, `Item_8026AE84`, `Item_80268E5C`, `it_8026D78C`, `it_8026E414`, `it_80272860`, `it_80276408`, and `it_8026B894`.

Reconstructed collision behavior around `CollData.env_flags`, including `MPCOLL_UNK` handling via `it_80276408` and left/right wall handling by negating `facing_dir`, cached horizontal velocity, and live x velocity.
Reconstructed air deceleration behavior in `it_80293C40`, using item fall speed attributes and `KinokoAttrs.x4` to reduce horizontal velocity toward zero.
Expanded `src/melee/it/items/itdkinoko.h` from only the externally referenced prototypes to all local state callback prototypes present in the C file.
smart_moves: Used existing `itkinoko.h` shared types, especially `KinokoAttrs`, instead of inventing a separate special-attributes layout for `itdkinoko`.

Mapped raw offsets from assembly into established item struct fields such as `pos.x`, `facing_dir`, `xD5C`, `x40_vel`, `xDD4_itemVar.kinoko`, `xC4_article_data`, `xCC_item_attr`, `ground_or_air`, and `x378_itemColl`.
Preserved exact state-transition and callback shape by reconstructing `it_803F6130` as two `ItemStateTable` entries matching the deleted data section.
Used named item flags such as `ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE`, `ITEM_UNK_0x1`, and `ITEM_HIT_PRESERVE` instead of raw constants where available.
Used named collision flags `MPCOLL_UNK`, `MPCOLL_RIGHTWALL`, and `MPCOLL_LEFTWALL` to explain bit tests originally expressed as PowerPC rotate/mask instructions.
Inserted `PAD_STACK(8)` and `PAD_STACK(16)` in functions where stack shape mattered for matching, showing a practical stack-padding tactic rather than overcomplicating the C logic.
decomp_lessons: Small item modules can often be matched by translating the state table first, then implementing each callback around existing item helper calls and shared item fields.
When assembly accesses item variable storage at offsets like `0xDD4` through `0xDE0`, checking for an existing union member such as `xDD4_itemVar.kinoko` can produce clearer matching C.
Raw constant callback arguments can often be represented with existing item flag names while still preserving the original bit patterns.
For item collision callbacks, `ground_or_air != GA_Air` gates are a direct way to express assembly checks against ground/air state before floor or wall collision handling.
Wall-bounce behavior in item code may require updating both facing direction and cached velocity fields, not only `x40_vel.x`, to keep later callbacks consistent.
Air friction/deceleration logic can be matched cleanly with explicit absolute-value logic instead of introducing library calls, avoiding unwanted codegen.
After an item file matches, both `configure.py` and `obj_files.mk` must be updated so the C object replaces the asm object in the build.
naming_conventions: Kept address-based symbol names such as `it_80293A08`, `it_80293A70`, `it_80293AE0`, `it_80293AE8`, `it_80293B24`, `it_80293BE8`, `it_80293C10`, `it_80293C38`, `it_80293C40`, `it_80293CF0`, `it_80293D6C`, and `it_80293D74` because no semantic names were evidenced.
Used `KinokoAttrs` and `xDD4_itemVar.kinoko` naming from the existing mushroom item infrastructure.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced `.sdata2` float constants `0.0f`, `-1.0f`, and `1.0f` with direct C float literals in initialization and velocity code.
Converted the deleted assembly data table at `it_803F6130` into a C `ItemStateTable` array with two rows and callback pointers.
Matched stack-sensitive functions with `PAD_STACK(8)` in `it_80293A70` and `PAD_STACK(16)` in `it_80293B24`.
Expressed PowerPC `rlwinm`/`clrlwi` collision flag checks as named bitmask tests against `cd->env_flags`.
Kept simple always-false and always-true callback functions as direct `return false;` and `return true;`, matching trivial `li r3, 0/1; blr` assembly.
Translated wrapper functions around `Item_80268E5C` and `it_8026B894` directly rather than inlining or reworking behavior.
Used explicit sign checks and negation for horizontal velocity instead of a math helper, matching the assembly branch structure for absolute value and deceleration.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename or semantically document `itdkinoko` functions after address-based matching.
Compare `itdkinoko.c` with `itkinoko.c` to identify reusable mushroom-item behavior and possible shared abstractions.
Search for additional item modules still using `xDD4_itemVar.kinoko` or similar item-var unions to guide future matching.
Check whether `MPCOLL_UNK` usage in item collision callbacks is later renamed or clarified.
